title: Reusable Client Architecture, Transport Abstractions, Middleware, and Configuration · Go Client Systems
description: A reusable Go SDK needs one configured Client, narrow RoundTripper seams, ordered middleware, typed operations, and rotating configuration. Learners cumulatively produce a Go 1.26 net/http small composable client library boundary through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Centralize base authority, transport, timeout, user agent, credentials, limits, and defaults in an explicit client configuration. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Inject the narrow request execution capability needed for deterministic tests without cloning the whole HTTP library. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Compose authentication, retries, metrics, tracing, caching, and redaction with documented ordering and ownership. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
